manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: jack, march, bond, diamond, switch, sub, cast, tablet, button.
Your opponent's team words are: nurse, note, glass, ray, drill, point, mass, revolution.
Distractor words are: time, slug, ice, pool, shadow, play, dragon.
The kill words are: snow.

We need to choose a clue that is semantically related to some of these words. The clue cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them. Also must be a single word.

We need to output:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

where TARGETS is a comma-separated list of the target words that the clue relates to.

We need to choose which words to target. The goal is to maximize the chance that partner guesses all team words before opponent. However, we need to consider risk: if we give a clue that relates to many words, the partner may guess incorrectly and hit opponent words or distractor or kill word. The kill word is "snow". Also there are opponent words: nurse, note, glass, ray, drill, point, mass, revolution. Distractor words: time, slug, ice, pool, shadow, play, dragon.

We need to pick a clue that is related to as many of our team words as possible, but not too broad that partner might guess opponent/distractor/kill words.

We need to think about semantic grouping.

Our team words: jack, march, bond, diamond, switch, sub, cast, tablet, button.

Potential associations:

- "jack" can be a playing card, a tool, a name, a type of plug, a device.

- "march" can be a month (March), a procession, a military march, a piece of music.

- "bond" can be a connection, a spy (James Bond), a financial bond.

- "diamond" can be a gemstone, a shape, a suit in cards.

- "switch" can be a device to turn on/off, a change.

- "sub" can be a submarine, a substitute, a sub sandwich, a subscription.

- "cast" can be a group of actors, to throw, a plaster cast.

- "tablet" can be a pill, a slab of stone, a computer tablet.

- "button" can be a clothing fastener, a UI element, a button you press.

We need a single word that ties multiple of these together.
